channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Advice for 2026 commencement speakers: Don't bring up AI
item.description: Commencement speakers who bring up the sweeping changes that artificial intelligence is driving are facing boos from the Class of 2026.
item.content:encoded:
Commencement speakers who bring up the sweeping changes that artificial intelligence is driving are facing boos from the Class of 2026.

(Image credit: University of Central Florida and Middle Tennessee State University via Storyful)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX IPO plans reveal blockbuster spending on rockets and AI
item.description: The company is on track to pull off the largest IPO in history — making CEO Elon Musk even wealthier.
item.content:encoded:
The company is on track to pull off the largest IPO in history — making CEO Elon Musk even wealthier.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan

item.title: Supreme Court voting rights ruling could play a big role at the local level
item.description: The Supreme Court's recent ruling threatens the power of racial-minority voters in Voting Rights Act cases about not just Congress, but also at least 17 state and local governments, NPR finds.
item.content:encoded: The Supreme Court's recent ruling threatens the power of racial-minority voters in Voting Rights Act cases about not just Congress, but also at least 17 state and local governments, NPR finds.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: How to help children cope after shootings like the San Diego mosque killings
item.description: Children closest to an incident of gun violence have the most risk of lasting psychological effects. Here's what all parents should know about how to buffer trauma.
item.content:encoded:
Children closest to an incident of gun violence have the most risk of lasting psychological effects. Here's what all parents should know about how to buffer trauma.

(Image credit: Gregory Bull)
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee

item.title: The U.S. threatens to revoke the Palestinian U.N. ambassador's visa
item.description: A leaked State Department memo shows the U.S. threatened to revoke Palestinian visas if they pushed for a senior United Nations post.
item.content:encoded:
A leaked State Department memo shows the U.S. threatened to revoke Palestinian visas if they pushed for a senior United Nations post.

(Image credit: Angelina Katsanis)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Meta slashes 8,000 jobs as it pivots towards AI
item.description: Facebook and Instagram's parent company has been investing huge sums of money in AI, but it lags behind competitors.
item.content:encoded:
Facebook and Instagram's parent company has been investing huge sums of money in AI, but it lags behind competitors.

(Image credit: Jeff Chiu)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: After the Kars4Kids ad is banned in California, we check in on nostalgic jingles past
item.description: A California judge has given Kars4Kids 30 days to either take its ads off the air or update them to disclose its affiliations to a Jewish charity based in the Northeast.
item.content:encoded: A California judge has given Kars4Kids 30 days to either take its ads off the air or update them to disclose its affiliations to a Jewish charity based in the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: U.S. grand jury indicts Raul Castro, ex-Cuban president
item.description: The 94-year-old former leader of Cuba faces several charges, including four counts of murder for an attack on a humanitarian group more than 30 years ago.
item.content:encoded:
The 94-year-old former leader of Cuba faces several charges, including four counts of murder for an attack on a humanitarian group more than 30 years ago.

(Image credit: Alexandre Meneghini)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: Bipartisan home affordability bill passes the House
item.description: The bill is meant to encourage home construction, and would ban corporate investors from buying up more homes to rent out.
item.content:encoded:
The bill is meant to encourage home construction, and would ban corporate investors from buying up more homes to rent out.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: The Justice Department gives Trump an unprecedented settlement
item.description: D.O.J. gave Trump and his family immunity from tax audits and set up a $1.8 billion fund for victims of "government weaponization." Former government lawyer Andrew Weissmann explains the settlement.
item.content:encoded: D.O.J. gave Trump and his family immunity from tax audits and set up a $1.8 billion fund for victims of "government weaponization." Former government lawyer Andrew Weissmann explains the settlement.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross
